findings.summary: Concurrent routed full-auto saves can lose earlier writes because canonical merge preparation happens before the folder lock is acquired.
findings.recommended_fix: Move canonical merge preparation under the folder lock or re-run merge generation inside the locked section before promotion.

findings.summary: Resume precedence is freshness-first in code but handover-first in the documented contract and bootstrap messaging.
findings.recommended_fix: Either enforce true handover-first selection in the helper or update all resume-facing docs and tests to the freshness-winner rule.

findings.summary: Gate D still documents archived and legacy fallback even though the live resume handler explicitly disables both.
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite Gate D to the shipped three-level no-archive contract, or reintroduce the archived stage in runtime and tests.

findings.summary: Gate E teaches parent-handover recovery through ../handover.md, but the live helper only checks handover.md inside the resumed folder.
findings.recommended_fix: Either implement parent-handover traversal for phase-child resumes or remove the ../handover.md claim from Gate E docs.

findings.summary: The save mutex is process-local only, so cross-process full-auto saves remain unguarded if multiple runtimes target the same packet.
findings.recommended_fix: Document single-process limits explicitly or add an interprocess lock/lease for canonical full-auto saves.

findings.summary: Key save and filter guarantees are still only partially verified because the routed concurrency test is skipped and Gate D filtering checks are TODO.
findings.recommended_fix: Promote the skipped/TODO cases into blocking integration coverage before further save or resume refactors land.

findings.summary: The active parent packet points at a canonical research path that does not exist, breaking packet-local research discoverability.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the parent packet to the actual packet-local research root and keep that breadcrumb in sync with future research runs.

findings.summary: The 004-memory-save-rewrite packet still self-identifies as 014-memory-save-rewrite, conflicting with the flattened parent phase map.
findings.recommended_fix: Normalize packet-local metadata and summaries to 004, keeping any old 014 value as an alias field only if external references still need it.
